questions.question: What does datetime.date(2024, 12, 25) create?
questions.explanation: datetime.date(year, month, day) creates a date object representing just the date — no time component.

questions.question: Which strftime code represents a 4-digit year?
questions.explanation: %Y gives the full 4-digit year (e.g. 2024). %y gives just the last 2 digits (e.g. 24).

questions.question: What does strptime do?
options: Converts a date to a formatted string
Parses a string into a datetime object
questions.explanation: strptime (string parse time) takes a string and a format code and converts it into a datetime object.

questions.question: Which class do you use for date arithmetic (adding or subtracting days)?
questions.explanation: timedelta represents a duration. You add or subtract it from date or datetime objects to calculate new dates.

questions.question: Complete: datetime.______.today() returns the current date.
What does datetime.datetime(2024, 12, 25, 10, 30) represent?
questions.explanation: datetime.datetime() with year, month, day, hour, minute creates a full datetime object. hour and minute are optional and default to 0.
